title: Privacy Policy
introDesc: mqvi is an open-source communication platform. We are committed to transparency about what data we collect and how we handle it. This policy applies to the mqvi instance hosted at mqvi.net.
dataItem1: Account information: username, display name, and optionally your email address (used only for password recovery).
dataItem2: Messages: text messages you send in servers and direct messages are stored on our servers to deliver them to recipients. Where end-to-end encryption is enabled, we store only the encrypted form and cannot read the content.
dataItem3: Files: images, videos, and other files you upload are stored on our servers for delivery.
dataItem4: Basic usage data: timestamps of account creation and last activity for operational purposes.
dataItem5: Push notification tokens: if you enable notifications on mobile, a device token issued by Apple or Google is stored to deliver them. It is removed when your account is deleted.
dataUsageDesc: Your data is used solely to provide the service:
usageItem1: Deliver messages and files to their intended recipients.
usageItem2: Send password reset emails if you provided an email address.
noTrackingDesc: We do not use cookies for tracking, analytics services, advertising networks, or any form of user profiling. There are no third-party trackers embedded in mqvi.
dataSharingDesc: We do not sell, rent, or share your data with third parties. Your data is only accessible to the recipients you communicate with. Two narrow exceptions exist to operate the service: push notifications are delivered through Apple (APNs) and Google (FCM) infrastructure, and GIF searches are forwarded to our GIF provider without any account identity attached.
selfHostDesc: When you create a self-hosted server on mqvi, only the voice and video infrastructure runs on your own LiveKit server. Your messages, direct messages, files, and account data are still stored on mqvi.net and covered by this policy. If you want full control over all data, you can fork the open-source project and run your own independent instance — in that case, the privacy practices are determined by that instance's operator.
deletionDesc: You can delete your account in the app under Settings → Security. Your account is deactivated immediately and can be restored within a 30-day grace period by logging in again with your password. After 30 days, your profile information, uploaded files, push tokens, and personal data are permanently removed. Your messages are preserved for conversation continuity but displayed anonymously as "Deleted User". Direct message conversations remain visible to the other participant.
contactDesc: For privacy-related questions, open an issue on our GitHub repository or reach out via the app.
delIntro: You can delete your mqvi account directly in the app — no email or support request needed.
delStep1: Open the mqvi app and sign in.
delStep2: Open Settings and go to the Security section.
delStep3: Under Delete account, tap the delete button.
delStep4: Confirm the deletion in the dialog.
delData1: Grace period: your account is deactivated immediately. For 30 days you can restore it by logging in again with your password.
delData2: After 30 days: your profile information, uploaded files, push notification tokens, and personal data are permanently deleted.
delData3: What is kept: your past messages remain for conversation continuity but are shown anonymously as "Deleted User". Direct message conversations remain visible to the other participant.
delPartialTitle: Delete some data without deleting your account
delPartialIntro: You can also remove individual pieces of data at any time, directly in the app:
delPartial1: Messages: delete any message you sent (hover over it and choose Delete on desktop, long-press on mobile). Deleted messages are permanently removed from the server, including any attached files.
delPartial2: Email address: your email is optional — you can remove it from your account under Settings → Security.
delPartial3: Profile data: your avatar, wallpaper, and profile details can be changed or removed under Settings at any time.
